info.name: ClearSky Revenue Model
info.description:
All API endpoints for the ClearSky revenue model — Layer 1 (GBP Health Gap), Layer 2 (Local Rank Gap), individual calculation verification, and full GBP Gap breakdown.

Base URL: https://clearskysoftware.net (override {{baseUrl}} variable for local dev).

item.name: 1. Full Pipeline — Layer 1 & Layer 2

request.description: One-click test — static body matches revenue-model-layer1 POST JSON. Same fields as fetch: businessName, city, trade, yearsInBusiness, websiteUrl, psiScore, avgSaleValue, mapPackPosition. Full pipeline: Google Places → GBP gap → modifiers → 15% discount.

One-click test — static body matches revenue-model-layer2 POST JSON. Same fields as destructuring: businessName, city, trade, secondaryTrade, revenueSplit, websiteUrl, yearsInBusiness, annualRevenue, avgSaleValue, avgSaleValueSecondary. Full pipeline: Google light → DataForSEO rank → PageSpeed → diagnostic.

item.description: These are the main pipeline endpoints that run the FULL calculation including live API calls to Google, DataForSEO, and PageSpeed Insights.

item.name: 2. Variable Verification (No API Calls)
Benchmark Volumes — Sudbury keyword volumes per trade

request.description:
Returns the hardcoded Sudbury benchmark search volumes per keyword.

Plumbing total = 339 (287+26+20+3+3)
HVAC total = 219 (96+52+27+27+17)

These are the base volumes before city scaling.
HVAC benchmark volumes. Total = 219.

item.name: City Households — scaling factor for city

request.description:
Returns household count, scaling factor (households ÷ 73,000), and captive market uplift (× 1.20) for any city.

Also lists ALL known city clusters so you can compare.

Calculates the 9-signal GBP composite score (0–100) and shows each signal's earned vs max points.

Also returns:
- penalty = min((100 − score) ÷ 100, 0.85)
- throughput = 1 − penalty (this is what gets multiplied in the formula)

Converts PSI performance score to site retention rate.

Accepts mobile + desktop (auto-averaged) or direct psiScore.

Lookup table:
90–100 → 1.00 (Green)
70–89 → 0.90 (Amber)
50–69 → 0.85 (Amber)
0–49 → 0.80 (Red)

item.name: Ranking CTR — map pack position lookup

request.description:
Returns CTR for a map pack position and the gap from #1.

Key rule: If #1, raw gap = 0 but FLOOR of 0.15 is applied (always some opportunity).

Pos 1 → CTR 0.44, gap 0.15 (floor)
Pos 2 → CTR 0.24, gap 0.20
Pos 3 → CTR 0.17, gap 0.27
None → CTR 0.03, gap 0.41

item.name: Tenure Rate — years in business lookup

request.description:
Returns the brand tenure rate for years in business.

16+ yrs → +16% (Legacy)
11–15 → +9% (Trusted)
6–10 → +4% (Established)
2–5 → −5% (Building)
0–1 → −15% (New)

item.name: Economic Rate — city market tier lookup

request.description:
Returns the economic rate for a city or market tier.

Booming → +15%
Active → +5%
Neutral → 0%
Slow → −15%
Depressed → −30%

Applies tenure and economic modifiers to a base value, then the 15% ultra-conservative discount.

Example: baseValue 24960 × (1 + 0.09 + 0.05) × 0.85

item.description: These endpoints return lookup values and run pure calculations with NO external API calls. Use them to verify every variable in the GBP Gap formula independently.

item.name: 3. Full GBP Gap Breakdown (Step-by-Step)
GBP Gap Breakdown — plumbing, Timmins, #1 for primary

request.description:
THE MAIN VERIFICATION ENDPOINT.

Runs the full formula per keyword showing every intermediate value:

GBP Gap = sudburyVolume × (households ÷ 73,000) × 1.20
  × max(0.44 − currentCTR, 0.15)
  × (1 − min((100 − compositeScore) ÷ 100, 0.85))
  × siteRetentionRate × 0.10
  × avgSaleValue × 12
  × (1 + tenureRate + economicRate) × 0.85

Each keyword runs independently. Results are summed, then modifiers and discount applied.

item.name: GBP Gap Breakdown — HVAC, Timmins, not ranked

request.description: Same formula but for HVAC trade with $3000 avg sale value. All keywords not ranked (position "none").

item.name: GBP Gap Breakdown — plumbing, Sudbury (index city)

request.description:
Sudbury = index city (73,000 households), so scaling factor = 1.0 × 1.20 = 1.20.
Mixed positions: #1, #2, #3, and not ranked to see different CTR gaps.

item.name: GBP Gap Breakdown — auto defaults (no keywords/score provided)

request.description:
Minimal request — uses all defaults:
- Benchmark Sudbury volumes
- All keywords position "none"
- compositeScore 0 (no GBP data)
- PSI 100 (full retention)
- Default avgSaleValue for trade
- 5 years in business

item.description: The gbp-gap-breakdown endpoint is the KEY verification tool. It runs the complete GBP Gap formula per keyword and shows every intermediate value so you can follow the math.

item.name: 4. Individual API Data Endpoints (Live Calls)

request.description: Google Places lookup — light mode returns CID, rating, reviewCount, name, website.
Google Places lookup — full mode includes photos, hours, Q&A, services, editorial summary.
Fetches live keyword search volume from DataForSEO for "trade city" query.

Combined DataForSEO lookup: Q&A, search volume, and GBP services.

NOTE: Replace the identifier with the actual CID or place_id from the google-identity response.

item.name: Rank Lookup — SERP positions for 5 keywords

request.description: Fetches SERP rank positions for a business across 5 locked keywords using DataForSEO.
Runs Google PageSpeed Insights and returns performance score + site retention rate.
Pure engine calculation for Layer 1 GBP gap — no API calls. Pass in the data directly.

item.name: Weighted Rank Gap — Layer 2 engine calculation

request.description: Pure engine calculation for Layer 2 weighted rank gap — no API calls. Pass in the rank data directly.
Runs the full diagnostic engine calculation (all layers combined). Pure engine, no API calls.

item.description: Individual endpoints for each step in the pipeline. Some call external APIs (Google, DataForSEO), some are pure engine calculations.
